title: Aureline system-browser callback, credential-store lock-state, and account-boundary vocabulary
description: Boundary schema for the system-browser auth callback, credential-store lock state, and local/self-hosted/managed account-boundary packet frozen in docs/auth/system_browser_callback_packet.md. Three record kinds are exported: auth_callback_packet_record (outbound handoff + pending session + return route + callback correlation + preserved-local-work note + recovery path), credential_store_lock_state_record (available / locked / denied / unavailable / degraded / policy_blocked plus export-safe copy and retry-path labels), and account_boundary_record (local / self-hosted / managed posture plus the passkey-capable, reauth-required, seat-loss, and deprovision-preserves-local-work rows later managed-auth work extends rather than replaces). Raw tokens, raw provider URLs, raw provider codes, raw session cookies, raw client-certificate bytes, raw callback query strings, and raw passkey material never cross this boundary; every record carries opaque refs, typed vocabulary, and export-safe summary labels only. Live OAuth implementation and provider-specific integrations are out of scope at this revision. Adding a new auth-flow class, callback-failure reason, credential-store lock state, retry path, account-boundary class, reserved-row kind, browser-launch policy class, or audit-event id is additive-minor and requires an auth_callback_state_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row co-signed by security_trust_review and the owning lane.

auth_callback_state_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this auth callback, credential-store lock-state, and account-boundary export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape.

instance_schema_hint.description: Optional $schema pointer carried on worked fixtures.

fixture_metadata.description: Optional worked-example prelude carried on fixtures.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, and safe in support/export packets. Raw tokens, raw provider URLs, raw cookies, raw codes, and raw callback query strings never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

export_safe_label.description: Short, reviewable label safe to render on banners, support exports, release evidence, and audit streams. Raw secret material, raw URLs, raw query strings, raw codes, and raw cookies MUST NOT appear.

identity_mode.description: Identity mode re-exported from ADR-0001. account_free_local is the default; self_hosted_org speaks OIDC/SCIM against a customer-run IdP over the system browser; managed_workspace is the vendor-hosted convenience layer.

account_boundary_class.description: Closed account-boundary vocabulary. local_only never requires an account or network. self_hosted uses a customer-run IdP. managed is vendor-hosted convenience. restricted_managed_only is a managed posture where a particular org policy narrows the surface to managed-sign-in-required. grace_degraded_managed is a bounded posture where managed services are unreachable but local and self-hosted capability stay truthful. unknown_boundary is admissible only as a fail-closed state routed to repair.

deployment_profile_class.description: Frozen deployment-profile vocabulary reused across auth boundary, credential store, and support/export surfaces.

workspace_trust_state.description: Workspace trust state re-exported from ADR-0001. Auth boundary rows never silently widen trust on callback return.

auth_flow_class.description: Closed auth flow-class vocabulary re-exported from ADR-0015. system_browser is the default and only primary path; embedded_password_exception is never the default happy path and requires an active exception-register row.

browser_launch_policy_class.description: Closed browser-launch policy vocabulary. system_default_browser_required is the default; embedded webviews are never a silent fallback. managed_approved_browser_allowed is the narrow case where a managed policy pins a specific approved browser binary over the system default. separately_approved_boundary_contract is admissible only when a co-signed boundary contract replaces the system-browser default. browser_launch_policy_blocked names the visible-recovery fail-closed state.

callback_return_mode_class.description: Closed callback return-mode vocabulary. Loopback and deep link return are typed paths with origin/tenant/workspace validation; device_code_poll_return is the return channel for the device-code fallback; manual_return_resume is the last-resort path when no automated return path exists. not_applicable is used by non-callback auth rows such as pure passkey step-up.

state_token_alias.description: Alias for the state-token the host minted. Raw state-token bytes never appear here; the alias is the export-safe projection.

nonce_alias.description: Alias for the nonce bound into the outbound request. Raw nonce bytes never appear here.

pkce_challenge_alias.description: Alias for the PKCE challenge / verifier pair when the flow used PKCE. Null when the flow did not use PKCE (for example, device-code poll return).

bound_workspace_ref.description: Opaque ref to the originating Aureline workspace the pending session is bound to. Null only for pre-workspace sign-in flows.

bound_tenant_or_org_ref.description: Opaque ref to the tenant or org scope the pending session is bound to. Null when the identity mode is account_free_local or when the surface does not pin a tenant.

bound_actor_subject_ref.description: Opaque ref to the acting actor subject the pending session is bound to. Null when the flow does not pre-bind a subject.

replay_posture.description: Replay posture of the correlation envelope. Callback replay is denied on any replay.

redeemed_at.description: When the callback was successfully redeemed. Null while pending, denied, or failed.

callback_receipt_ref.description: Opaque ref to the callback-redemption receipt that the support/export, release-evidence, and mutation-journal surfaces quote by stable id.

callback_correlation.description: Correlation envelope binding an outbound browser handoff to the pending sign-in session, the originating workspace/tenant/subject scope, and a single-use redemption receipt. Raw tokens, raw nonces, and raw PKCE verifiers never appear here; only typed aliases the broker resolves at use time.

return_anchor_ref.description: Opaque ref to the stable anchor the user returns to after the browser hop. Round-trips independently of provider query-string behavior.

return_target_label.description: Export-safe label naming where the user returns (for example, 'Aureline desktop – payments-prod workspace'). Raw URLs MUST NOT appear.

return_origin_validation_class.description: Closed origin-validation vocabulary for the return path. strict_origin_match_required is the default for any HTTPS loopback or platform deep-link return; device_code_poll_only and manual_resume_only name the return channels without a browser-originated callback.

return_tenant_or_workspace_match_rule.description: Typed rule describing which binding a returning browser state must match before the callback mutates state. no_tenant_or_workspace_binding is only admissible for pre-workspace account_free_local sign-in.

return_policy_check_refs.description: Refs to the policy-check rows the return path must satisfy before the callback redeems. Empty is admissible for account_free_local.

return_route.description: Return-route envelope naming the mode, anchor, target label, origin-validation class, tenant/workspace match rule, and referenced policy checks the callback must satisfy. Raw URLs, raw query strings, and raw cookies never appear here.

posture_class.description: Closed posture vocabulary naming what happens to local editing, save, undo, search, Git, tasks, local model execution, and BYOK AI while sign-in is incomplete, failed, or paused. local_work_blocked_by_policy is admissible only as a visibly-degraded fail-closed state with a typed repair hook; it is never a silent default.

retained_capabilities.description: What still works locally while the browser hop is pending, failed, or returning. Editing / save / undo / search / local Git / local tasks / local AI remain representable without implying network or account.

blocked_capabilities.description: What is narrowed while sign-in is incomplete. Empty is admissible for account_free_local.

preserved_local_work.description: Required preserved-local-work block. Copy-only shadow versions of this block on surfaces are forbidden; banners, activity center, support packets, and admin exports quote this block by reference.

visible_recovery_required.description: True when callback failure, pending-session mismatch, or credential-store locked state MUST surface a visible recovery path. False is admissible only when the auth lane has no pending browser hop (for example, pre-sign-in account_free_local).

repair_hook_ref.description: Opaque ref to a typed repair hook row a support/diagnostics surface can quote. Null when the recovery action is self-contained.

recovery_path.description: Required recovery-path envelope naming the primary and fallback actions, visibility requirement, reviewable copy, and optional typed repair hook. Surfaces never invent their own retry vocabulary.

retry_path_class.description: Closed retry-path vocabulary for callback failures and credential-store denials.

credential_store_class.description: Credential-store class vocabulary re-exported from ADR-0007.

credential_store_lock_state_class.description: Closed credential-store lock-state vocabulary for the boundary packet. available means the preferred store is unlocked and usable. locked means the store is known and present but not currently unlocked. denied means a typed broker denial refused the request (class rule, consumer capability mismatch, tool-reveal denial, remote scope mismatch, approval ticket missing). unavailable means the store is reachable-but-broken, corrupted, or missing. degraded corresponds to the ADR-0007 degraded_session_only posture. policy_blocked means a policy bundle or profile enforcement denies the class, store, or operation at this policy epoch.

credential_store_denial_reason_class.description: Closed denial-reason vocabulary re-exported from the ADR-0007 secret-broker denial-reason set.

credential_store_copy_catalog.description: Required copy catalog naming the banner headline, plain-language summary, retry action, secondary action, and export-safe status label the support/export, release-evidence, and admin-export surfaces quote by id.

reserved_row_kind.description: Closed reserved-row vocabulary so later managed-auth work extends the boundary packet instead of replacing it. passkey_capable names that the flow can step up with a platform authenticator without leaving the system-browser-first posture. reauth_required is the state after session expiry, password reset, policy epoch roll, or trust downgrade. seat_loss is the state when managed entitlement is lost (license seat removed, org membership lost). deprovision_preserves_local_work is the durable state guaranteeing that local workspace, local Git, local tasks, and BYOK AI keep working after managed deprovision.

row_state.description: Closed state vocabulary for reserved rows. reserved_for_future_row is the seed posture before managed-auth work lands; supported_capability_declared marks the capability as available; active_state marks the row as currently in effect; not_applicable_in_this_posture is admissible for local-only boundary rows.

preserves_local_work.description: Explicit preserves-local-work assertion for this row. True is required for deprovision_preserves_local_work.

repair_hook_ref.description: Opaque ref to a typed repair hook for active or expiring row states.

reserved_row.description: One reserved-row entry. Present so later managed-auth work extends the same boundary packet instead of minting a parallel contract.

audit_event_id.description: Closed audit-event id vocabulary for the system-browser auth, credential-store, and account-boundary lane. Audit rows carry typed vocabulary only; raw tokens, raw URLs, raw cookies, raw codes, raw passkey material, and raw provider query strings are forbidden.

policy_context.description: Policy, identity-mode, and trust-state context the record was minted under.

redaction_class.description: Redaction class re-exported from the lifecycle/support/release vocabulary.

pending_session_state_class.description: Closed pending-session state vocabulary. return_denied_* states fail closed; silent fallback to an embedded webview or to a wrong tenant/workspace context is forbidden.

reserved_rows.description: Reserved rows for passkey_capable, reauth_required, seat_loss, and deprovision_preserves_local_work. At least one entry for each row_kind MUST be present so later managed-auth work extends the same packet instead of replacing it.

browser_handoff_packet_ref.description: Opaque ref to the ADR-0010 browser_handoff_packet that carries the outbound handoff. Null is admissible only for not_applicable and device_code_poll_return variants.

origin_disclosure_ref.description: Opaque ref to the canonical Aureline origin the pending session was minted from. Consumers validate this ref on every callback redemption.

embedded_fallback_posture.description: Embedded-fallback posture. embedded_fallback_forbidden is the default and required value for system_browser, device_code, and platform_authenticator_native rows. embedded_session_refresh_allowed_if_declared and embedded_password_exception_allowed_if_declared are admissible only with an active exception-register row.

pending_session_denied_reason.description: Typed denial reason when pending_session_state is one of the return_denied_* / session_expired / session_superseded states. Null while pending or completed.

allOf.description: system_browser auth flow requires system_default_browser_required or managed_approved_browser_allowed launch policy unless a separately_approved_boundary_contract is named.
device_code auth flow MUST use device_code_poll_return and forbids embedded fallback.
platform_authenticator_native is host-native or browser/platform-owned; never an embedded auth page.
account_free_local identity mode requires local_only boundary and MUST NOT name a bound tenant subject.
Pending-session denied states must name a typed denial reason.
Reserved rows MUST cover each of the four reserved row kinds at least once.

auth_callback_packet_record.description: Auth callback packet record. One record per pending or returning sign-in session. Carries the outbound handoff class, the return-route and origin/tenant/workspace match rule, the callback-correlation envelope (state alias, nonce alias, PKCE alias, bound workspace/tenant/subject), the preserved-local-work block, the recovery path, the system-browser / device-code launch posture, and the four reserved rows later managed-auth work extends. Raw tokens, raw codes, raw cookies, raw provider URLs, and raw query strings never appear.

denial_reason_class.description: Typed denial reason when lock_state_class is denied or policy_blocked. Null otherwise.

retained_local_capabilities.description: What still works locally while the credential store is locked, denied, unavailable, degraded, or policy-blocked. Editing, save, undo, search, local Git, local tasks, local model execution, and BYOK AI MUST remain representable without implying credential access.

affects_pending_callback_refs.description: Refs to pending auth_callback_packet_record ids this lock state narrows or invalidates. Empty when no pending session is affected.

session_fallback_posture.description: Session-fallback posture re-exported from ADR-0007 when lock_state_class is degraded. Null otherwise.

allOf.description: denied and policy_blocked states require a typed denial reason.
degraded state requires a declared session-fallback posture.
Non-available lock states require visible recovery.

credential_store_lock_state_record.description: Credential-store lock-state record. One record per store-class posture, carrying the closed lock-state vocabulary (available, locked, denied, unavailable, degraded, policy_blocked), the ADR-0007 denial-reason and session-fallback refs, an export-safe copy catalog, a typed recovery path, and the retained/blocked local capabilities so support / release-evidence / admin-export surfaces resolve one shared object instead of inventing copy.

visible_downgrade_path.description: Required visible-downgrade path naming which boundary class the posture narrows to when org or credential state narrows capability, and whether local work is preserved.

reserved_rows.description: Reserved rows for passkey_capable, reauth_required, seat_loss, and deprovision_preserves_local_work. At least one entry for each row_kind MUST be present.

allOf.description: account_free_local identity mode requires local_only boundary and preserves_local_work on the downgrade path.
Reserved rows MUST cover each of the four reserved row kinds at least once.
deprovision_preserves_local_work reserved row MUST assert preserves_local_work=true.

account_boundary_record.description: Account-boundary record. One record per boundary posture (local_only, self_hosted, managed, restricted_managed_only, grace_degraded_managed, unknown_boundary). Carries the identity-mode binding, deployment-profile scope, export-safe boundary label, plain-language summary, the visible downgrade path, the preserved-local-work block, and the four reserved rows (passkey_capable, reauth_required, seat_loss, deprovision_preserves_local_work) later managed-auth work extends.
